celebration: Leaf #6 — the wh leaf COMPLETES the digraph branch (sh · ch · th · ck/FLSZ · ng · wh). 🎉 QUESTION WEEK: the five question words go up together — when and which fully decodable, and what, where and why arrive as the class's first heart words since Level 1 (why is promised to earn its own letters at Week 55).

songs.title: Wh-Wh-When?
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
Wh! Wh! Wh-wh-wh!
(wh… wh… wh…)

[Hook]
Wh! Wh! Wh-wh-wh!
It asks a question!
Wh-wh-when? Wh-wh-when?
WHEN is it? (when? when?)

[Verse 2]
Wh! Wh! Wh-wh-wh!
Round your lips and blow!
Wh-wh-which? Wh-wh-which?
WHICH is it? (which? which?)

[Verse 3]
Wh! Wh! Wh-wh-wh!
What? Where? Why?
The wh words ask and ask!
What? (what!) Where? (where!) Why? (why!)

[Verse 4]
Wh! Wh! Wh-wh-wh!
Whip and whiz — so quick!
Wh-wh-whiz! Wh-wh-whip!
Whiz! (zoom!) Whip! (snap!)

[Bridge]
A potato?! (NO!)
P-p-potato! Not WH!
(wh… wh… wh…)

[Outro]
When? Which? What? Where? Why?
The wh words ask! (ask! ask!)
Wh! Wh! Wh-wh-wh!
(who… knows…)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: wh makes the /w/ sound in most accents — round the lips and blow a little air — never the letter names 'double-u aitch'. This is the QUESTION week: 'when' and 'which' are fully decodable, and 'what', 'where', 'why' enter as HEART words taken on faith ('why' earns its letters far ahead at Week 55). Verse 3 chants the three heart words as a rising question drum-roll. Sing every wh word as a question that lifts at the end. Never sing letter names.

songs.title: Which Is It?
songs.lyrics:
[Intro]
(We can read. We can read!)

[Hook]
Which is it? Which is it?
What is it? Where is it?
Which is it? (look! look!)
Which is it?

[Verse 2]
When can Chick get it?
Whiz! It went so quick!
Which is it? (which? which?)
When? (now! now!)

[Verse 3]
What is it? Where is it?
Why is it so quick?
Which is it? (which? which?)
When? (now! now!)

[Outro]
Which… when… what… where… why…
A potato?! (POTATO!)
THAT is it! (that! that!)
songs.sunoStyle: dark trap, 68 bpm, heavy 808 bass, sparse hi-hats, deep whisper-rap verses, kids choir chant on hook, playful spooky, minimal, clean vocals, nursery trap
songs.sunoNotes: Frame: 'Which is it?' — a guessing game in song form. Verses fire every question word (which/when + heart words what/where/why). Keep the phrasing as real questions rising at the end. The reveal in the outro is the potato — the class shouts it, then lands 'THAT is it!' as the answer.

spreads.artNote: A bright streak of motion-blur shoots across the dark spotlight — something has zipped past too fast to see. A single 'whiz' line trailing sparks. Mystery established.
Chick spinning around wide-eyed, wings up, question marks popping over her head. She missed it too.
Sheep squinting thoughtfully at three fuzzy shapes in the shadows, one hoof raised. The guessing game begins.
A coiled whip revealed in a pool of light — one candidate. Chick leans in to inspect. 'No?'
The little wooden ship from Week 27 sails into a beam of light — another candidate. Sheep shakes its head. 'No?'
The bright orange fish flips into the light — a third candidate. Chick tilts her head. 'Hmm, no?'
Chick and Sheep back to back scanning every corner of the dark stage, tiny flashlight beams crossing. The thing is nowhere to be found.
Three streaks of blur crisscross the spread at once — the mystery thing zooming in circles around the pair, who spin trying to track it. Peak chaos.
The whizzing finally stops: a single potato has skidded to a halt centre-stage, tiny skid marks behind it, looking innocent. Chick and Sheep gawk. No noun printed — the class SHOUTS it.

extras: PATTERN TREE: leaf #6 — the wh leaf COMPLETES the digraph branch. The class reads whip/when/whiz/which aloud and the first branch is full.

QUESTION-WORD CEREMONY: the five question words go up on a special board — when and which on green 'decodable' cards, what/where/why on gold 'heart' cards (taken on faith). Tell the class why (the word!) will earn its own letters at Week 55 — a promise, like Snake's name-day.
wh makes /w/: round the lips and blow a whisper of air. Contrast with plain w (wet/whet, wig/whig oral) — same sound, and that's fine; the spelling is the new thing.
QUESTION GAME: hold up an object behind your back — the class asks 'What is it? Which is it? Where is it?' using the new words. Moveable alphabet: build wh onto whip, when, whiz.

teacherFocus: THE QUESTION WEEK, and the last of the digraphs. wh makes /w/ — round the lips and blow a whisper of air (in most accents it is identical to plain w, which is fine; the spelling is the new thing). Two of the wh question words are decodable (when, which); three are HEART words the class takes on faith (what, where, why) — put them up on gold cards and promise the children that 'why' will earn its own letters at Week 55, exactly like Snake's name-day. Play the question game all week: hide an object and let the class interrogate it — 'What is it? Which is it? Where is it?' Hang leaf #6 and step back: the whole first branch of the Pattern Tree is now full.
